input.voice: Sharp, sardonic, data-driven. Product managers and UX professionals.
input.rewards: 3 referrals = monthly live data teardown; 10 referrals = one-year paid subscription
input.subscriber_profile: Mid-to-senior product managers and UX leads at tech companies and agencies

test_cases.output_summary: 130-word referral section. Opens with voice-appropriate description of ideal recipient (colleague who over-cites Nielsen Norman). Rewards presented as clean two-line summary. Pre-written share copy is sardonic and sounds like something a product manager would actually send. Referral link placeholder clearly labelled.

input.voice: Thoughtful, melancholy, literary. Serious book people.
input.subscriber_profile: People who work in or around publishing, or read seriously

test_cases.output_summary: 100-word organic sharing section. No reward framing. Ask is specific: 'someone who notices when a novel's sentences are doing something unusual'. Pre-written share copy reads like a genuine recommendation, not a marketing blurb. Low-pressure close. Tone maintains the newsletter's literary register throughout.

input.voice: Methodical, precise, no advocacy. Sustainability professionals and financial analysts.
input.rewards: 5 referrals = quarterly emissions-discrepancy database access; 15 referrals = annual paid subscription
input.subscriber_profile: ESG analysts, sustainability officers, climate journalists

test_cases.output_summary: 160-word section. No hype language. Ideal recipient described in precise professional terms ('a colleague trying to reconcile contradictory ESG disclosures'). Rewards presented as simple two-tier structure. Pre-written share copy is factual and professional — reads like a genuine analyst recommendation. Referral link placeholder included.
